name: acmecse-dev

metadata.name: acmecse-dev
metadata.summary: An open source CSE Middleware for Education

documentation:
# ![](https://github.com/ankraft/ACME-oneM2M-CSE/blob/4e6b4878ffc1a1295f443a243f21d269bf745fd0/acme/webui/web/img/acme_sm.png?raw=true) 

# ACME oneM2M CSE
An open source CSE Middleware for Education.

![](https://github.com/ankraft/ACME-oneM2M-CSE/blob/4e6b4878ffc1a1295f443a243f21d269bf745fd0/docs/images/title.png?raw=true)
## Introduction

This oneM2M compliant CSE implements a subset of the oneM2M standard (see [http://www.onem2m.org](http://www.onem2m.org)). The intention is to provide an easy to install, extensible, and easy to use and maintainable CSE for educational purposes.


## Installing
Install with pip or your favorite PyPI package manager.

	python -m pip install acmecse

## Running

Run the following to run the ACME CSE with the current directory as the runtime directory:

	python -m acmecse

To specify a different runtime directory, use the `-dir` option:

	python -m acmecse -dir /path/to/runtime

A configuration file and necessary directories will be created in the runtime directory if they do not exist.

## Documentation

See [https://acmecse.net](https://acmecse.net) for more exhaustive information.

## License

BSD 3-Clause License for the CSE and its native components and modules. Please see the individual licenses of the used third-party components.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.STTE.STTE.willBeDeactivated
documentation: 2) If the request is attempting to update the stateTransitions attribute, the Receiver shall check if after processing the request, the value of the stateActive attribute of the <state> resource will equal “False”. If not, the receiver shall return a response primitive with a Response Status Code indicating an "OPERATION_NOT_ALLOWED" error.

3) The Receiver shall check the existence and accessibility of an <action> child resource, if any, referenced by the stateAction attribute of the request. If the referenced <action> child resource does not exist, does not have retrieve privileges for the Originator, or is not a child resource of this <state> resource, then the Receiver shall return a response primitive with a Response Status Code indicating an "INVALID_PROCESS_CONFIGURATION" error.

4) The Receiver shall check the existence and accessibility of any <state> resources referenced by the stateTransitions attribute of the request. If any of the referenced <state> child resources do not exist, do not have retrieve privileges for the Originator or are not child resources of the parent <processManagement> resource, then the Receiver shall return a response primitive with a Response Status Code indicating "INVALID_PROCESS_CONFIGURATION" error.

5) For any evalCriteria defined in the stateTransitions attribute of the request, the Receiver shall check the existence and accessibility of any resource and attribute referenced by the subject element of the evalCriteria. If any of the referenced resources or attributes do not exist or do not have retrieve privileges for the Originator, then the Receiver shall return a response primitive with a Response Status Code indicating an "INVALID_PROCESS_CONFIGURATION" error.

6) For any evalCriteria defined in the stateTransitions attribute of the request, the Receiver shall check the value provided for the threshold element of the evalCriteria attribute is within the value space (as defined in [3]) of the data type of the subject element of the evalCriteria attribute. The Receiver shall also check that the value provided for the operator element of the evalCriteria attribute is a valid value based on Table 6.3.4.2.86-1. If either check fails, the receiver shall return a response primitive with a Response Status Code indicating an "INVALID_PROCESS_CONFIGURATION" error.

functions.name: acmecse.resources.Factory.resourceFromDict
documentation:
Create a resource from a dictionary structure.

This function will **not** call the resource's *activate()* method, therefore some attributes
may need to be set separately.

Args:
        resDict: Dictionary with the resource definition.
        pi: The parent's resource ID.
        ty: The resource type of the resource that shall be created.
        create: The resource will be newly created.
        isImported: True when the resource is imported, or created by the `ScriptManager`. In this case some checks may not be performed.
        template: True when the resource is used as a template. In this case some checks may not be performed.

Return:
        `Result` object with the *resource* attribute set to the created resource object.

functions.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.addToInternalAttributes
documentation:
Add a *name* to the names of internal attributes. 

*name* is only added if it is not already present.

Args:
        name: Attribute name to add.
Internal helper functions

functions.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.isInternalAttribute
documentation:
Check whether an attribute is an internal attribute.

Args:
        name: Attribute name to check.
Return:
        True if the attribute is an internal attribute.

variables.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.internalAttributes
documentation: List of internal attributes and which do not belong to the oneM2M resource attributes

classes.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.__init__
documentation:
Initialization of a Resource instance.

Args:
        ty: Mandatory resource type.
        dct: Mandatory resource attributes.
        pi: Optional parent resource identifier.
        typeShortname: Optional domain and resource type short name.
        create: Optional indicator whether this resource is just created or an instance of an existing resource.
        inheritACP: Optional indicator whether this resource inherits *acpi* attribute from its parent (if any).
        readOnly: Optional indicator whether this resource is read-only.
        rn: Optional resource name. If none is given and the resource is created, then a random name is assigned to the resource.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.asDict
documentation:
Get the JSON resource representation.

Args:
        embedded: Optional indicator whether the resource should be embedded in another resource structure. In this case it is *not* embedded in its own "domain:name" structure.
        update: Optional indicator whether only the updated attributes shall be included in the result.
        noACP: Optional indicator whether the *acpi* attribute shall be included in the result.

Return:
        A `JSON` object with the resource representation.
Default encoding implementation. Overwrite in subclasses

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.activate
documentation:
This method is called to activate a resource, usually in a CREATE request.

This is not always the case, e.g. when a resource object is just used temporarly.
**NO** notification on activation/creation happens in this method!

This method is implemented in sub-classes as well.

Args:
        parentResource: The resource's parent resource.
        originator: The request's originator.

Raises:
        `BAD_REQUEST`: In case of an invalid attribute.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.willBeDeactivated
documentation:
This method is called before a resource will be deactivated.

This method is implemented in some sub-classes, which may throw an
execption if the resource cannot be deactivated. If it is implemented
it should call the super class' `willBeDeactivated()` method to check
for child resources.                    

Args:
        originator: The request originator.
        parentResource: The resource's parent resource.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.deactivate
documentation:
Deactivate an active resource.

This usually happens when creating the resource via a request.
A subscription check for deletion is performed.

This method is implemented in sub-classes as well.

Args:
        originator: The requests originator that let to the deletion of the resource.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.update
documentation:
Update, add or remove resource attributes.

A subscription check for update is performed.

This method is implemented in sub-classes as well.

Note:
        This method updates the resource in the database. It should be called only after all other checks where performed.

Args:
        dct: An optional JSON dictionary with the attributes to be updated.
        originator: The optional requests originator that let to the update of the resource.
        doValidateAttributes: If *True* optionally call the resource's `validate()` method.

Raises:
        `CONTENTS_UNACCEPTABLE`: In case of a resource mismatch.
        `BAD_REQUEST`: In case of an invalid attribute.
        `INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR`: In case the parent resource coudln't be retrieved.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.willBeUpdated
documentation:
This method is called before a resource will be updated and before calling the `update()` method.

This method is implemented in some sub-classes.

Args:
        dct: `JSON` dictionary with the attributes that will be updated.
        originator: The request originator.
        subCheck: Optional indicator that a blocking Update shall be performed, if configured.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.updated
documentation:
Signal to a resource that is was successfully updated. 

This handler can be used to perform     additional actions after the resource was updated, stored etc.

This method is implemented in some sub-classes.

Args:
        dct: Optional JSON dictionary with the updated attributes.
        originator: The optional request originator.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.willBeRetrieved
documentation:
This method is called before a resource will be send back in a RETRIEVE response.

This method is implemented in some sub-classes.

Args:
        originator: The request originator.
        request: The RETRIEVE request.
        subCheck: Optional indicator that a blocking Retrieve shall be performed, if configured.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.childWillBeAdded
documentation:
Called before a child will be added to a resource.

This method is implemented in some sub-classes.

Args:
        childResource: Resource that will be added as a child to the resource.
        originator: The request originator.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.childAdded
documentation:
Called after a child resource was added to the resource.

This method is implemented in some sub-classes.

Args:
        childResource: The child resource that was be added as a child to the resource.
        originator: The request originator.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.childUpdated
documentation:
Called when a child resource was updated.
                
This method is implemented in some sub-classes.

Args:
        childResource: The child resource that was be updates.
        updatedAttributes: JSON dictionary with the updated attributes.
        originator: The request originator.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.childRemoved
documentation:
Called when a child resource of the resource was removed.

        This method is implemented in some sub-classes.

Args:
        childResource: The removed child resource.
        originator: The request originator.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.canHaveChild
documentation:
Check whether *resource* is a valild child resource for this resource. 

Args:
        resource: The resource to test.
Return:
        Boolean indicating whether *resource* is a an allowed resorce for this resource.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.validate
documentation:
Validate a resource. 

Usually called within `activate()` or `update()` methods.

This method is implemented in some sub-classes.

Args:
        originator: Optional request originator
        dct: Updated attributes to validate
        parentResource: The parent resource

Raises
        `BAD_REQUEST`: In case of a validation error.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.isCreatedInternally
documentation:
Test whether a resource has been created for another resource.

Return:
        True if this resource has been created for another resource.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.setCreatedInternally
documentation:
Save the resource ID for which this resource was created for.

This has some impacts on internal handling and checks.

Args:
        ri: Resource ID of the resource for which this resource has been created for.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.isAnnounced
documentation:
Test whether a the resource's type is an announced type. 

Returns:
        True if the resource is an announced resource type.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.isVirtual
documentation:
Test whether the resource is a virtual resource. 

Return:
        True when the resource is a virtual resource.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.handleRetrieveRequest
documentation:
Process a RETRIEVE request that is directed to a virtual resource.

This method **must** be implemented by virtual resource class.

Args:
        request: The request to process.
        id: The structured or unstructured resource ID of the target resource.
        originator: The request's originator.

Return:
        Result object indicating success or failure.
request handler stubs for virtual resources

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.handleCreateRequest
documentation:
Process a CREATE request that is directed to a virtual resource.

This method **must** be implemented by virtual resource class.

Args:
        request: The request to process.
        id: The structured or unstructured resource ID of the target resource.
        originator: The request's originator.
Return:
        Result object indicating success or failure.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.handleUpdateRequest
documentation:
Process a UPDATE request that is directed to a virtual resource.

This method **must** be implemented by virtual resource class.

Args:
        request: The request to process.
        id: The structured or unstructured resource ID of the target resource.
        originator: The request's originator.
Return:
        Result object indicating success or failure.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.handleDeleteRequest
documentation:
Process a DELETE request that is directed to a virtual resource.

This method **must** be implemented by virtual resource class.

Args:
        request: The request to process.
        id: The structured or unstructured resource ID of the target resource.
        originator: The request's originator.
Return:
        Result object indicating success or failure.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.handleNotification
documentation:
Process a notification request that is directed to a resource.

This method is implemented in some sub-classes. Those implementations
override this method to handle the notification request.

Args:
        request: The request to process.
        originator: The request's originator.

Raises:
        `INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR`: In case the method is not implemented and overridden in a sub-class.
Other directed request handling

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.setAttribute
documentation:
Assign a value to a resource attribute.

If the attribute doesn't exist then it is created.

Args:
        key: The resource attribute's name. This can be a path (see `setXPath`).
        value: Value to assign to the attribute.
        overwrite: Overwrite the value if already set.
Attribute handling

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.attribute
documentation:
Return the value of an attribute.

Args:
        key: Resource attribute name to look for. This can be a path (see `findXPath`).
        default: A default value to return if the attribute is not set.
Return:
        The attribute's value, the *default* value, or None

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.hasAttribute
documentation:
Check whether an attribute exists for the resource.

Args:
        key: Resource attribute name to look for.
Return:
        Boolean, indicating the existens of an attribute

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.delAttribute
documentation:
Delete the attribute 'key' from the resource. 

Args:
        key: Name of the resource attribute name to delete.
        setNone:  By default (*True*) the attribute is not deleted but set to *None* and later removed 
                          when storing the resource in the DB. If *setNone' is *False*, then the attribute is immediately
                          deleted from the resource instance's internal dictionary.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.getAttributes
documentation:
Get all attributes of the resource. 

Args:
        includingInternal: Optional indicator whether internal attributes shall be included.

Return:
        Dictionary with a copy of all attributes.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.getFinalResourceAttribute
documentation:
Determine and return the final value of an attribute during an update.

Args:
        key: Attribute name.
        dct: The dictionary with updated attributes.

Return:
        The either updated attribute, or old value if the attribute is not updated. The method returns *None* if the attribute does not exists.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.__setitem__
documentation:
Implementation of the *self[key]* operation for assigning to attributes.

It maps to the `setAttribute()` method, and always overwrites existing values.

Args:
        key: The resource attribute's name. This can be a path (see `setXPath`).
        value: Value to assign to the attribute.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.__getitem__
documentation:
Implementation of the *self[key|* operation for retrieving attributes.

It maps to the `attribute()` method, but there is no default value.

Args:
        key: Resource attribute name to look for. This can be a path (see `findXPath`).
Return:
        The attribute's value, or None

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.__delitem__
documentation:
Implementation of the *self[key|* operation for deleting attributes.

It maps to the `delAttribute()` method, with *setNone* implicitly set to the default.

Args:
        key: Resource attribute name to delete. This can be a path (see `findXPath`).

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.__contains__
documentation:
Implementation of the membership test operator.

It maps to the `hasAttribute()` method.

Args:
        key: Resource attribute name to test for.
Return:
        Boolean, indicating the existens of an attribute

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.__getattr__
documentation:
Map the normal object attribute access to the internal resource attribute dictionary.

It maps to the `attribute()` method, but there is no default value.

Args:
        key: Resource attribute name to get.
Return:
        The attribute's value, or None

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource._normalizeURIAttribute
documentation:
Normalize the URLs in the given attribute.

Various changes are made to the URI in case they are not fully compliant.
This could be, for example, *poa*, *nu* and other attributes that usually hold a URI.

If the target attribute is a list of URI then all the URIs in the list are normalized.

Args:
        attributeName: Name of the attribute to normalize.
Attribute specific helpers

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource._checkAndFixACPIreferences
documentation:
Check whether a referenced `ACP` resoure exists, and if yes, change the ID in the list to CSE relative unstructured format.

Args:
        acpi: List if resource IDs to `ACP` resources.

Return:
        If fully successful (ie. all `ACP` resources exist), then a new list with all IDs converted is returned.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.hasAttributeDefined
documentation:
Test whether a resource supports the specified attribute.
This method may be overwritten in sub-classes, for example for virtual resources.

Args:
        name: Attribute to test.
Return:
        Boolean with the result of the test.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.dbDelete
documentation:
Delete the resource from the database.

Return:
        Result object indicating success or failure.
Database functions

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.dbUpdate
documentation:
Update the resource in the database.

This also raises a CSE internal *updateResource* event.

Args:
        finalize: Treat this database write as a final update to the resource. Only then an event is raised.

Return:
        Result object indicating success or failure.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.dbCreate
documentation:
Add the resource to the database.

Args:
        overwrite: If true an already existing resource with the same resource ID is overwritten.
Return:
        Result object indicating success or failure.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.dbReload
documentation:
Load a **new** copy of the same resource from the database. 

The current resource is NOT changed. 

Note:
        The version of the resource in the database might be different, e.g. when the resource instance has been modified but not updated in the database.

Return:
        Resource instance.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.dbReloadDict
documentation:
Reload the resource instance from the database.

The current resource's internal attributes are updated with the versions from the database.

Return:
        Updated Resource instance.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.__str__
documentation:
String representation of the resource's attributes.

Return:
        String with the resource formatted as a JSON structure
Misc utilities

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.__repr__
documentation:
Object representation as string.

Return:
        String that identifies the resource.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.__eq__
documentation:
Test for equality of the resource to another resource.

Args:
        other: Other object to test for.
Return:
        If the *other* object is a Resource instance and has the same resource ID, then *True* is returned, of *False* otherwise.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.structuredPath
documentation:
Determine the structured path of a resource.

Return:
        Structured path of the resource or None

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.isModifiedAfter
documentation:
Test whether this resource has been modified after another resource.

Args:
        otherResource: Another resource used for the test.
Return:
        True if this resource has been modified after *otherResource*.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.retrieveParentResource
documentation:
Retrieve the parent resource of this resouce.

Return:
        The parent Resource of the resource.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.retrieveParentResourceRaw
documentation:
Retrieve the raw (!) parent resource of this resouce.

Return:
        Document of the parent resource

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.getOriginator
documentation:
Retrieve a resource's originator.

Return:
        The resource's originator.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.getCurrentOriginator
documentation:
Retrieve the current originator. This could be different from
the originator / creator of the resource in case of the *custodian* attribute is set.

Return:
        The current originator.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.setOriginator
documentation:
Set a resource's originator.

This is the originator that created the resource. It is stored internally within the resource.

Args:
        originator: The originator to assign to a resource.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.setResourceName
documentation:
Set the resource name. 

Also set/update the internal structured resource name.

Args:
        rn: The new resource name for the resource.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.getSrn
documentation:
Retrieve a resource's full structured resource name.

Return:
        The resource's full structured resource name.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.setSrn
documentation:
Set a resource's full structured resource name.

Args:
        srn: The full structured resource name to assign to a resource.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.getRVI
documentation:
Retrieve a resource's release version indicator.

Return:
        The resource's *rvi*.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.setRVI
documentation:
Assign the release version for a resource.

This is usually assigned from the *rvi* indicator in the resource's CREATE request.

Args:
        rvi: Original CREATE request's *rvi*.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.getLocationCoordinates
documentation:
Retrieve a resource's location coordinates (internal attribute).

Return:
        The resource's location coordinates. Might be None.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.setLocationCoordinates
documentation:
Set a resource's location coordinates (internal attribute).

Args:
        crd: The location coordinates to assign to a resource.

methods.name: acmecse.resources.Resource.Resource.selectAttributes
documentation:
Determine the selected attributes for a partial retrieve of a resource.

Args:
        attributeList: The list of attributes to filter.


Raises:
        BAD_REQUEST: In case an attribute is not defined for the resource.

modules.name: acmecse.etc.DateUtils
documentation: This module contains various utilty functions that deal with dates and times.
DateUtils.py
(c) 2021 by Andreas Kraft
License: BSD 3-Clause License. See the LICENSE file for further details.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.DateUtils.getResourceDate
documentation:
Generate an UTC-relative ISO 8601 timestamp and return it.

Args:
        offset: adds or substracts *offset* seconds to the generated timestamp.
Return:
        String with UTC-relative ISO 8601 timestamp.
Time, Date, Timestamp related

functions.name: acmecse.etc.DateUtils.toISO8601Date
documentation:
Convert and return a UTC-relative float timestamp or datetime object to an ISO 8601 string.

Args:
        ts: Timestamp. Either a POSIX float timestamp, of a *datetime* object.
        readable: Optional boolean indicating whether the output should be in the format "YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss,f
Return:
        ISO 8601 datetime string.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.DateUtils.fromISO8601Date
documentation:
Convert an ISO 8601 date time string to a *datetime* object.

Args:
        date: ISO 8601 datetime string.
Return:
        Datetime object.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.DateUtils.fromAbsRelTimestamp
documentation:
Parse a ISO 8601 string and return a UTC-based POSIX timestamp as a float.

If the *absRelTimestamp* is a period (relative) timestamp (e.g. "PT2S"), then this function
tries to convert it and return an absolute POSIX timestamp as a float, based on the current UTC time.

If the *absRelTimestamp* contains a stringified integer then it is treated as a relative offset in ms and
a UTC-based POSIX timestamp is generated for this offset and returned.

Args:
        absRelTimestamp: A string with either a stringified integer that represents a POSIX UTC-based timestamp,
                or a ISO 8601 period timestamp.
        default: The default that is returned in case a conversion fails.
        withMicroseconds: The function handles the timestamps including micro seconds.
Return:
        A float representing a UTC-based POSIX timestamp.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.DateUtils.fromDuration
documentation:
Convert a duration to a number of seconds (float). 

Args:
        duration: String with either an ISO 8601 period or a string with a number of ms.
        allowMS: If True, the function tries to convert the string as if it contains a number of ms.
Return:
        Float, number of seconds.
Raise:
        Exception if wrong format is provided in *duration*.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.DateUtils.toDuration
documentation:
Convert a POSIX timestamp to ISO 8601 duration format.

Args:
        ts: Float with a UTC-based POSIX time stamp.
Return:
        A string with an ISO 8601 duration.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.DateUtils.rfc1123Date
documentation:
Return a date time string in RFC 1123 format, e.g. for use in HTTP requests.

The timestamp is UTC-based.

Args:
        timeval: Optional UTC-based POSIX timestamp to use, otherwise the current time is used.
Return:
        String with the UTC-based time.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.DateUtils.utcDatetime
documentation:
Return the current datetime, but relative to UTC.

Returns:
        Datetime with current UTC-based time.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.DateUtils.utcTime
documentation:
Return the current time's timestamp, but relative to UTC.

Returns:
        Float with current UTC-based POSIX time.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.DateUtils.timeUntilTimestamp
documentation:
Return the time in seconds until the UTC-based POSIX timestamp is reached.

Args:
        ts: UTC-based POSIX timestamp.

Return:
        Number of seconds until the timestamp. A negative value means that the timestamp lies is the past.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.DateUtils.timeUntilAbsRelTimestamp
documentation:
Return the time in seconds until the UTC-based POSIX *absRelTimestamp* is reached.

Args:
        absRelTimestamp: String with either an ISO 8601 period or a string with an 
                integer (UTC-based POSIX timestamp in seconds).

Return:
        Time in seconds until the timestamp is reached. Negative values mean that the timestamp lies is the past.
        0.0 is returned in case of an error.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.DateUtils.isodateDelta
documentation:
Calculate the delta between and ISO 8601 date time string and a UTC-based POSIX timestamp.

Args:
        isoDateTime: ISO 8601 compatible string.
        now: Optional float with a time stamp. If *None* is given then the current time (UTC-based) will be taken.

Return:
        A signed float value indicating the delta (negative when the given ISO date time 
        is earlier then "now"), or *None* in case of an error.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.DateUtils.waitFor
documentation:
Busy waiting for *timeout* seconds, or until a *condition* callback function returns *True*.

Args:
        timeout: The time to wait at most for the event to be true. 
        condition: A callback. It it returns *True* the wait-for condition is met and the waiting stops. 
                If it is None, then only the *timeout* is used, and *False* is always returned.
        latency: The time between *condition* checks.

Return:
        The functionn returns *True* if the *condition* returns *True*  
        before the timeout is reached, and *False* otherwise.
        It returns *False* if *timeout* is negative.
        If *condition* is not callable then *False* is returned.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.DateUtils.cronMatchesTimestamp
documentation:
A cron parser to determine if the *cronPattern* matches for a given timestamp *ts*.

The cronPattern must follow the usual crontab pattern of 7 fields:

        second minute hour dayOfMonth month dayOfWeek year

which each must comply to the following patterns:

- \* : any integer value
- \*/num : step values
- num[,num]\* : value list separator (either num, range or step)
- num-num : range of values

see also: https://crontab.guru/crontab.5.html

Args:
        cronPattern: Either a string with the pattern or a list of strings, one for each pattern element.
        ts: Optional timestamp. If *None* then a current UTC-based timestamp is used to fill the timestamp.

Return:
        Boolean, indicating whether time pattern matches the given timestamp.

Raises:
        ValueError: If *cronPattern* is invalid.
Cron

functions.name: acmecse.etc.DateUtils.cronInPeriod
documentation:
A parser to check whether a cron pattern has been true during a certain time period. 

This is useful for applications which cannot check every minute or need to catch up during a
restart, or want to determinethe next run at some time in the future.

Attention:
        Be aware that this function just tries every minute between *startTs* and *endTs*, 
        so it might take some time to execute.

Args:
        cronPattern: Either a string with the pattern or a list of strings, one for each pattern element.
        startTs: Start UTC-based POSIX timestamp.
        endTs: End UTC-based POSIX timestamp. If *None* then the current UTC time is used to fill the timestamp. 
                In this case *startTs* must be before *endTs*.

Return:
        Tupple[bool, datetime]. The first element indicates whether the *cronPattern* matches
        any time in the given period. The second element provides the matched timestamp.

Raises:
        ValueError: If *cronPattern* is invalid.

methods.name: acmecse.protocols.CoAPServer.ACMECoAPRequestLayer.receive_request
documentation:
Handle a CoAP request.

This method overrides the `receive_request` method of the CoapthonRequestLayer class to handle the requests.

Args:
        transaction: The CoAP transaction.

Returns:
        The transaction.

Implementation of an own request layer handler for the coapthon CoAP framework.

This implementation replaces the `receive_request` method of the RequestLayer class to handle the requests
according to the ACME CSE requirements. It doesn't call a resource layer to handle the requests, but an internal
handler.

methods.name: acmecse.protocols.CoAPServer.CoAPServer.unpause
documentation: Continue handling requests.

methods.name: acmecse.protocols.CoAPServer.CoAPServer.sendCoAPRequest
documentation:
Send a CoAP request to a URL.

Args:
        request: The request to send.
        url: The URL to send the request to.
        isDirectURL: Flag whether the URL is a direct URL.

Returns:
        The result of the request.

class_variables.name: acmecse.protocols.CoAPServer.CoAPServer.__slots__
documentation: Define slots for instance variables.
CoAPServer Server implementation.

modules.name: acmecse.etc.ACMEUtils
documentation: This module provides various utility functions.
ACMEUtils.py
(c) 2020 by Andreas Kraft
License: BSD 3-Clause License. See the LICENSE file for further details.
This module contains various utilty functions that are used from various
modules and entities of the CSE.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.ACMEUtils.isUniqueRI
documentation:
Test whether a resource ID does not yet exists.

Args:
        ri: Resource ID to check
Return:
        Boolean indicating the result of the test

functions.name: acmecse.etc.ACMEUtils.structuredPathFromRI
documentation:
Get the structured path of a resource by its ri.

Args:
        ri: Resource ID.
Return:
        Structured path, or None in case of an error.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.ACMEUtils.riFromStructuredPath
documentation:
Get the resource ID from a resource by its structured path. 
Makes a lookup to a table in the DB.

Args:
        srn: structured path.
Return:
        Resource ID, or None in case of an error.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.ACMEUtils.srnFromHybrid
documentation:
Get the structured part of a hybrid resource ID, including the necessary handling of virtual
resources in the path.

Args:
        srn: Structured version of a resource ID. This part will be filled in when ommitted.
        id: Resource ID to check.
Return:
        Tuple of the (possible new & filled) structured path and the resource ID.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.ACMEUtils.getIDFromPath
documentation:
Split a full path e.g. from a http request into its component and return a CSE local ri .
Also handle retargeting paths.

Args:
        id: A resource ID to process. This could be a structured or unstructured, and in CSE-relative, SP-relative or Absolute format.
Return:
        The return tupple is (RI, CSI of the resource ID, structured path of the ID, debug message or None).

functions.name: acmecse.etc.ACMEUtils.riFromCSI
documentation:
Get the resource ID from any CSEBase or remoteCSE resource by its csi.

Args:
        csi: The CSE-ID to search for.
Return:
        The resource ID of the resource with the *csi*, or None in case of an error.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.ACMEUtils.compareIDs
documentation:
Compare two resource IDs.

Both IDs can be either unstructured or structured resource IDs. They match
if they point to the same resource.

Args:
        id1: First ID for the comparison.
        id2: Second ID for the comparison
Return:
        True if both IDs point to the same resource, False otherwise.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.ACMEUtils.riFromID
documentation:
Return the unstructured resource ID from either an unstructured or structured resource ID.

Args:
        id: Structured or unstructured Resource ID.
Return:
        Unstructured resource ID.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.ACMEUtils.pureResource
documentation:
Return the "pure" structure without the "<domain>:xxx" resource type name, and the oneM2M type identifier. 

Args:
        dct: JSON dictionary with the resource attributes.
Return:
        Tupple with the inner JSON, the resource type name, and the found key.
        If the resource type name is not in the correct format, eg the domain is missing, it is *None*.
        The third element always contains the found outer attribute name.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.ACMEUtils.resourceDiff
documentation:
Compare an old and a new resource. A comparison happens for keywords and values.
Attributes which names start and end with "__" (ie internal attributes) are ignored.

Args:
        old: Old resource dictionary to compare.
        new: New resource dictionary to compare.
        modifiers: A dictionary. If this dictionary is given then it contains the changes that let from old to new. This is used to determine if attributes were just updated with the same values.
Return: 
        Return a dictionary of identified changes.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.ACMEUtils.resourceModifiedAttributes
documentation:
Calculate the difference between an original resource and after it has been updated, and then remove the attributes
that are part of the update request.

Args:
        old: Old resource dictionary to compare.
        new: New resource dictionary to compare.
        modifiers: A dictionary. If this dictionary is given then it contains the changes that let from old to new. This is used to determine if attributes were just updated with the same values.
Return: 
        Return a dictionary of those attributes that have been changed in a CREATE or UPDATE request.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.ACMEUtils.resourceFromCSI
documentation:
Get A CSEBase resource by its csi. This might be a different <CSEBase> resource then the hosting CSE.

Args:
        csi: *CSE-ID* of the <CSEBase> resource to retrieve.

Return:
        <CSEBase> resource or None if not found.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.ACMEUtils.getAttributeSize
documentation:
Return a realistic size for the content of an attribute.
Python does not really return good sizes for some of the data types.

Args:
        attribute: An attribute's content of any of the suppported types.
Return:
        Byte size of the attribute's value.

variables.name: acmecse.etc.ACMEUtils._excludeFromRoot
documentation: Attributes that are excluded from the root of a resource tree.

variables.name: acmecse.etc.ACMEUtils._pureResourceRegex
documentation: Regular expression to test for a pure resource name.

modules.name: acmecse.etc
documentation: This package provides various CSE-related utilitiy modules.

modules.name: acmecse.etc.IDUtils
documentation: IDUtils.py
(c) 2024 by Andreas Kraft
License: BSD 3-Clause License. See the LICENSE file for further details.
This module contains various utilty functions to handle, test and manipulate IDs.
There are no ACME CSE internal dependencies. This heelps to keep the module small and
standalone.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.IDUtils.uniqueRI
documentation:
Generate a unique resource ID. Beside a random number it
can have a prefix.

Args:
        prefix: Prefix for the ID
Return:
        String with the new ID
Identifier and path related

functions.name: acmecse.etc.IDUtils.uniqueID
documentation:
Generate a unique ID. This is for the moment just a large random number.
NO check for uniqueness is done.

Return:
        String with the identifier

functions.name: acmecse.etc.IDUtils.uniqueRN
documentation:
Generate a unique resource name. A resource name has a prefix and 
a random alpha-numeric string.

Args:
        prefix: String prefix. If it contains a domain then that is removed
Return:
        String with the resource name

functions.name: acmecse.etc.IDUtils.uniqueAEI
documentation:
Create a new AE ID. An AE ID must always start with either "S" or "C".

Args:
        prefix: "S" or "C"
Return:
        String with the AE ID
create a unique aei, M2M-SP type

functions.name: acmecse.etc.IDUtils.noNamespace
documentation:
Remove the namespace part of an identifier and return the remainder.

Example: 
        'm2m:cnt' -> 'cnt'

Args:
        id: String with the identifier. May be prefixed with a domain.
Return:
        String that only contains the ID

functions.name: acmecse.etc.IDUtils._randomID
documentation:
Generate an ID. Prevent certain patterns in the ID.

Return:
        String with a random ID

functions.name: acmecse.etc.IDUtils.localResourceID
documentation:
Test whether an ID is a resource ID of the local CSE.

Args:
        ri: A resource ID in CSE-relative, SP-relative, or absolute notation.
Return:
        If the ID targets a local resource then the CSE-relative form of the resource ID
        is returned, or None otherwise.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.IDUtils.isStructured
documentation:
Test whether a URI is in structured format.

Args:
        uri: The URI to check
Return:
        Boolean if the URI is in structured format

functions.name: acmecse.etc.IDUtils.isSPRelative
documentation:
Test whether a URI is SP-Relative. 

Args:
        uri: The URI to check
Return:
        Boolean

functions.name: acmecse.etc.IDUtils.isAbsolute
documentation:
Test whether a URI is in absolute format.

Args:
        uri: The URI to check
Return:
        Boolean if the URI is in absolute format

functions.name: acmecse.etc.IDUtils.isCSERelative
documentation:
Test whether a URI is in CSE-relative format.

Args:
        uri: The URI to check
Return:
        Boolean if the URI is in CSE-relative format

functions.name: acmecse.etc.IDUtils.toSPRelative
documentation:
Add the CSI to an originator (if not already present).

Args:
        id: A string with the originator or resource ID to convert.
Return:
        A string in the format */<csi>/<id>*.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.IDUtils.toCSERelative
documentation:
Convert an id to CSE-Relative format.

Args:
        id:     An ID in SP-relative or absolute format.
Return:
        An ID in CSE-Relative format.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.IDUtils.isValidCSI
documentation:
Test for valid CSE-ID format.

Args:
        csi: The CSE-ID to check
Return:
        Boolean

functions.name: acmecse.etc.IDUtils.isValidAEI
documentation:
Test for valid AE-ID format. 

It takes SP-Relative AEI's into account.

Args:
        aei: The AE-ID to check
Return:
        Boolean

functions.name: acmecse.etc.IDUtils.isValidID
documentation:
Test for a valid ID. 

Args:
        id: The ID to check
        allowEmpty: Indicate whether an ID can be empty.
Returns:
        Boolean

functions.name: acmecse.etc.IDUtils.isCSI
documentation:
Test whether a URI is a CSE-ID.

Args:
        uri: The URI to check

Return:
        Boolean if the URI is a CSE-ID

functions.name: acmecse.etc.IDUtils.hasOnlyUnreserved
documentation:
Test that an ID only contains characters from the unreserved character set of 
RFC 3986.

Args:
        id: the ID to check.
Returns:
        Boolean

functions.name: acmecse.etc.IDUtils.csiFromSPRelative
documentation:
Return the csi from a SP-relative resource ID. It is assumed that
the passed *ri* is in SP-relative format.

Args:
        ri: A SP-relative resource ID
Return:
        The csi of the resource ID, or None

functions.name: acmecse.etc.IDUtils.csiFromRelativeAbsoluteUnstructured
documentation:
Get the csi from an unstructured CSE-relative, SP-relative, or
absolute ID.

Args:
        id: unstructured ID.
Return:
        Tuple (CSE ID (no leading slashes) without any SP-ID or CSE-ID, list of path elements). If the ID is None or empty, the return value is ('', []).

functions.name: acmecse.etc.IDUtils.getIdFromOriginator
documentation:
Get AE-ID-Stem or CSE-ID from the originator (in case SP-relative or Absolute was used).

Args:
        originator: An originator.
        idOnly: Indicator that only the CSE-local resource ID is provided.
Returns:
        Resource ID.

variables.name: acmecse.etc.IDUtils._randomIDCharSet
documentation: Character set for random IDs.

variables.name: acmecse.etc.IDUtils._csiRx
documentation: Regular expression to test for valid CSE-ID format.
Must start with a / and must not contain a further / or white space

variables.name: acmecse.etc.IDUtils._aeRx
documentation: Regular expression to test for valid AE-ID format.
Must not start with a / and must not contain a further / or white space

variables.name: acmecse.etc.IDUtils._unreserved
documentation: Regular expression to test for unreserved characters.

class_variables.name: acmecse.etc.Constants.Constants.attrHasFCI

class_variables.name: acmecse.etc.Constants.Constants.attrImported

class_variables.name: acmecse.etc.Constants.Constants.attrLCPLink

class_variables.name: acmecse.etc.Constants.Constants.attrLocCoordinate

class_variables.name: acmecse.etc.Constants.Constants.attrModified

class_variables.name: acmecse.etc.Constants.Constants.attrNode

class_variables.name: acmecse.etc.Constants.Constants.attrOlRi
documentation: Constant: Name of the 'Resource internal *__olri__* attribute. This attribute holds the resourceID's of the *oldest* child-resource for some resource types.

class_variables.name: acmecse.etc.Constants.Constants.attrOriginator

class_variables.name: acmecse.etc.Constants.Constants.attrParentOriginator

class_variables.name: acmecse.etc.Constants.Constants.attrPCUAggregate

class_variables.name: acmecse.etc.Constants.Constants.attrPCURI

modules.name: acmecse.etc.Utils
documentation: This module provides various non-oneM2M related utility functions.
Utils.py
(c) 2020 by Andreas Kraft
License: BSD 3-Clause License. See the LICENSE file for further details.
This module contains various utilty functions that are used from various
modules and entities of the CSE.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.Utils.strToBool
documentation:
Convert a string value to a boolean.

Args:
        value: Any string value that looks like a boolean *true* or *false*.
Return:
        The boolean value.
Raises:
        ValueError if the string does not look like a boolean.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.Utils.runsInIPython
documentation:
Check whether the current runtime environment is IPython or not.

This is a hack!

Return:
        True if run in IPython, otherwise False.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.Utils.reverseEnumerate
documentation:
Reverse enumerate a list.

Args:
        data: List to enumerate.
Return:
        Generator that yields a tuple with the index and the value of the list.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.Utils.openFileWithDefaultApplication
documentation:
Open a file with the default application.

Args:
        filename: Name of the file to open.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.Utils.renameThread
documentation:
Rename a thread.

If *name* is provided then the thread is renamed to that name.
If *name* is not provided, but *prefix* is, then the thread is renamed to the prefix + the last 5 digits of its thread ID.
If neither *name* nor *prefix* is provided, then the thread is renamed to its own ID.

Args:
        name: New name for a thread. 
        thread: The Thread to rename. If none is provided then the current thread is renamed.
        prefix: Used for "prefix + ID" procedure explained above.

Returns:
        Always True.
Threads

functions.name: acmecse.etc.Utils.isURL
documentation:
Check whether a given string is a URL. 

Args:
        url: String to check for URL-ness.
Return:
        Boolean indicating whether the argument is a valid URL.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.Utils.isCoAPUrl
documentation:
Test whether a URL is a valid URL, and indicates a coap or coaps scheme.

Args:
        url: String to check.
Returns:
        True if the argument is a URL, and is an coap or coaps scheme.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.Utils.isHttpUrl
documentation:
Test whether a URL is a valid URL, and indicates an http or https scheme.

Args:
        url: String to check.
Returns:
        True if the argument is a URL, and is an http or https scheme.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.Utils.isMQTTUrl
documentation:
Test whether a URL is a valid URL, and indicates an mqtt URL. 

Args:
        url: String to check.
Returns:
        True if the argument is a URL, and is an mqtt or mqtts scheme.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.Utils.isWSUrl
documentation:
Test whether a URL is a valid URL, and indicates a WebSocket URL. 

Args:
        url: String to check.
Returns:
        True if the argument is a URL, and is a ws or wss scheme.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.Utils.isAcmeUrl
documentation:
Test whether a URL is a valid URL and an internal ACME event URL. 

Args:
        url: URL to check.
Returns:
        True if the argument is a URL, and is an internal ACME scheme.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.Utils.normalizeURL
documentation:
Remove trailing / from a url. 

Args:
        url: URL to remove trailing /'s from.
Returns:
        URL without trailing /'s.

variables.name: acmecse.etc.Utils._urlregex
documentation: Regular expression to test for a valid URL.

modules.name: acmecse.etc.RequestUtils
documentation: This module contains various utilty functions that are used to work with requests and responses.
RequestUtils.py
(c) 2021 by Andreas Kraft
License: BSD 3-Clause License. See the LICENSE file for further details.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.RequestUtils.serializeData
documentation:
Serialize a dictionary, depending on the serialization type.

Args:
        data: The data to serialize.
        ct: The *data* content serialization format.

Return:
        A data *str* or *byte* object with the serialized data, or *None*.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.RequestUtils.deserializeData
documentation:
Deserialize data into a dictionary, depending on the serialization type.

Args:
        data: The data to deserialize.
        ct: The *data* content serialization format.

Return:
        If the *data* is not *None*, but has a length of 0 then an empty dictionary is returned. If an unknown content serialization is specified then *None* is returned. Otherwise, a `JSON` object is returned.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.RequestUtils.toHttpUrl
documentation:
Make the *url* a valid http URL (escape // and ///) and return it.

Args:
        url: The URL to convert.

Return:
        A valid URL with escaped special characters.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.RequestUtils.fromHttpURL
documentation:
Make the *path* a valid oneM2M ID (unescape /~/ and /_/) and return it.
This is valid of CoAP URL paths as well.

Args:
        path: The path to convert.

Return:
        A valid ID with unescaped special characters.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.RequestUtils.toCoAPPath
documentation:
Make the *path* a valid CoAP URL path (escape / and //) and return it.

Args:
        path: The path to convert.

Return:
        A valid CoAP URL path with escaped special characters for oneM2M IDs

functions.name: acmecse.etc.RequestUtils.determineSerialization
documentation:
Determine the type of serialization for a notification from either the *url*'s *ct* query parameter,
or the given list of *csz* (contentSerializations, attribute of a target AE/CSE), or the CSE's default serialization.

As a side effect this function also validates the allowed URL scheme.

Args:
        url: The *URL* to parse.
        csz: The fallback content serialization.
        defaultSerialization: The CSE's defaults serialization.

Return:
        The determined content serialization, or *None* if none could be determined.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.RequestUtils.contentAsString
documentation:
Convert a content to a string. 
If the content is a string, it is returned as is.
If the content is a byte array, it is decoded to a string.
If the content is neither a string nor a byte array, it is converted to a hex string.

Args:
        content: The content to convert.
        ct: The content serialization type.

Return:
        The content as a string.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.RequestUtils.requestFromResult
documentation:
Convert a response request to a new *Result* object and create a new dictionary in *Result.data*
with the full Response structure. Recursively do this if the *embeddedRequest* is also
a full Request or Response.

Args:
        inResult: The input `Result` object.
        originator: The request originator.
        ty: Optional resource type.
        op: Optional request operation type
        isResponse: Whether the result is actually a response, and not a request.

Return:
        `Result` object with the response. The request or response is in *data*.

See Also:
        `responseFromResult`

functions.name: acmecse.etc.RequestUtils.prepareResultForSending
documentation:
Prepare a new request for MQTT or WebSocket sending. 

Attention:
        Remember, a response is actually just a new request. This takes care of the fact that in MQTT or WebSockets
        a response is very similar to a response.

Args:
        inResult: A `Result` object, that contains a request in its *request* attribute.
        isResponse: Indicator whether the `Result` object is actually a response or a request.
        originalRequest: The original request that was received.

Return:
        A tuple with an updated `Result` object and the serialized content.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.RequestUtils.responseFromResult
documentation:
Shortcut for `requestFromResult` to create a response object.

Args:
        inResult: Result that contains the response.
        originator: Originator for the response.

Return:
        `Result` object with the response.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.RequestUtils.createRawRequest
documentation:
Create a dictionary with a couple of pre-initialized fields. No validation is done.

Args:
        kwargs: individual attributes to set in the request.

Return:
        JSON dictionary with the request.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.RequestUtils.createPositiveResponseResult
documentation:
Create a positive `Result` object with a request.

Return:
        A `Result` object with a positive response.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.RequestUtils.createRequestResultFromURI
documentation:
Create a `Result` object from a URI. The URI is unquoted, parsed and the request is created.

Args:
        request: The request to create.
        url: The URL to parse.

Return:
        A tuple with the `Result` object, the URL and the parsed URL.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.RequestUtils.filterAttributes
documentation:
Filter a dictionary by a list of attributes to include.

Args:
        dct: Dictionary to filter.
        attributesToInclude: List of attributes to include.
        
Return:

functions.name: acmecse.etc.RequestUtils.removeNoneValuesFromDict
documentation:
Remove Null/None-values from a dictionary, but ignore the ones specified in *allowedNull*.

Args:
        jsn: JSON dictionary.
        allowedNull: Optional list of attribute names to ignore.
Return:
        Return a new dictionary with None-value attributes removed.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.RequestUtils.fillRequestWithArguments
documentation:
Fill a request with arguments from a `MultiDict`. The `MultiDict` contains the arguments from a request.

Args:
        arguments: The `MultiDict` with the arguments.
        dct: The dictionary to fill.
        cseRequest: The `CSERequest` object to fill.
        sep: The separator for the attribute list.

Return:
        The filled `CSERequest` object.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.RequestUtils.deserializeContent
documentation:
Deserialize a data structure.
Supported media serialization types are JSON and cbor.

Args:
        data: The data to deserialize.
        contentType: The content type of the data.

Return:
        The deserialized data structure.

Raises:
        *UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE* if the content type is not supported.
        *BAD_REQUEST* if the data is malformed.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.RequestUtils.curlFromRequest
documentation:
Create a cURL command from a request.

Args:
        request: The request to create the cURL command from.

Return:
        A cURL command.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.GeoTools.getGeoPoint
documentation:
Get the geo-point from a geoJSON object.

Args:
        jsn: The geoJSON object as a dictionary or a string.

Returns:
        A tuple of the geo-point (latitude, longitude). None if not found or invalid JSON.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.GeoTools.getGeoPolygon
documentation:
Get the geo-polygon from a geoJSON object.

Args:
        jsn: The geoJSON object as a dictionary or a string.

Returns:
        A list of tuples of the geo-polygon (latitude, longitude). None if not found or invalid JSON.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.GeoTools.isLocationInsidePolygon
documentation:
Check if a location is inside a polygon.

Args:
        polygon: The polygon as a list of tuples (latitude, longitude).
        location: The location as a tuple (latitude, longitude).

Returns:
        True if the location is inside the polygon, False otherwise.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.GeoTools.geoWithin
documentation:
Check if a shape is within another shape.

Args:
        aType: The type of the first shape.
        aShape: The shape of the first shape.
        bType: The type of the second shape.
        bShape: The shape of the second shape.
        
Returns:
        True if the first shape is (fully) within the second shape, False otherwise.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.GeoTools.geoContains
documentation:
Check if a shape contains another shape.

Args:
        aType: The type of the first shape.
        aShape: The shape of the first shape.   
        bType: The type of the second shape.    
        bShape: The shape of the second shape.  
        
Returns:
        True if the first shape (fully) contains the second shape, False otherwise.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.GeoTools.geoIntersects
documentation:
Check if a shape intersects another shape.

Args:
        aType: The type of the first shape.
        aShape: The shape of the first shape.   
        bType: The type of the second shape.    
        bShape: The shape of the second shape.  
        
Returns:
        True if the first shape intersects the second shape, False otherwise.

functions.name: acmecse.etc.GeoTools.getGeoShape
documentation:
Get a shapely geometry object from a geoJSON shape.

Args:
        typ: The geometry type.
        shape: The geoJSON shape as a tuple or list.

Returns:
        A shapely geometry object.

methods.name: acmecse.databases.DBBinding.DBBinding.searchResources
documentation:
Search for resources by structured resource name, resource ID, CSE-ID, parent resource ID, resource type,
or application entity ID.

Only one of the parameters may be used at a time. The order of precedence is: structured resource name,
resource ID, CSE-ID, structured resource name, parent resource ID, resource type, application entity ID.

Args:
        ri: A resource ID.
        csi: A CSE ID.
        srn: A structured resource name.
        pi: A parent resource ID.
        ty: A resource type.
        aei: An application entity ID.

Return:
        A list of found resource documents, or an empty list.

methods.name: acmecse.databases.DBBinding.DBBinding.discoverResourcesByFilter
documentation:
Search for resources by a filter function. This goes through all resources in the database and
applies the filter function to each resource. If the filter function returns True, the resource is added
to the result list.

Args:
        func: The filter function to use.

Return:
        A list of found resource documents, or an empty list.

methods.name: acmecse.databases.DBBinding.DBBinding.hasResource
documentation:
Check if a resource exists in the database.

Only one of the parameters may be used at a time. The order of precedence is: structured resource name,
resource ID, resource type.

Args:
        ri: A resource ID.
        srn: A structured resource name.
        ty: A resource type.

Return:
        True if the resource exists, False otherwise.

methods.name: acmecse.databases.DBBinding.DBBinding.countResources

methods.name: acmecse.databases.DBBinding.DBBinding.searchByFragment

modules.name: acmecse.databases.PostgreSQLBinding
documentation:
This module provides the database binding for PostgreSQL. It implements the
DBBinding interface and provides the necessary functions to interact with a
PostgreSQL database. The module uses the psycopg2 library to connect to the
database and execute SQL queries.
PostgreSQLBinding.py
(c) 2024 by Andreas Kraft
License: BSD 3-Clause License. See the LICENSE file for further details.
Database Binding for PostgreSQL

classes.name: acmecse.databases.PostgreSQLBinding.PostgreSQLBinding

methods.name: acmecse.databases.PostgreSQLBinding.PostgreSQLBinding.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the PostgreSQLBinding object.

Args:
        dbHost: The hostname of the database server.
        dbPort: The port of the database server.
        dbUser: The username to connect to the database.
        dbPassword: The password to connect to the database.
        dbDatabase: The name of the database to connect to.
        dbSchema: The schema to use in the database.

methods.name: acmecse.databases.PostgreSQLBinding.PostgreSQLBinding.createTables
documentation: Create the necessary schema and tables if they do not exist.

methods.name: acmecse.databases.PostgreSQLBinding.PostgreSQLBinding.upgradeTables
documentation: Upgrade the tables if necessary.

methods.name: acmecse.databases.PostgreSQLBinding.PostgreSQLBinding.prepareStatements
documentation:
Prepare the PreparedStatements for various SQL operations. 

This method is called after the database connection is established and
the tables are created. It prepares the SQL statements for the various
operations that can be performed on the database. This includes inserting,
updating, and deleting resources, identifiers, child resources, and
subscriptions.

Note that prepared statements are only usable within the same connection.
Therefore, this method should be called after the connection is established.

methods.name: acmecse.databases.PostgreSQLBinding.PostgreSQLBinding._checkOpenConnection
documentation:
Check if the database connection is open.

Try to reconnect if the connection is closed.

methods.name: acmecse.databases.PostgreSQLBinding.PostgreSQLBinding._executePrepared
documentation:
Execute a prepared statement.

This is the main method to execute a prepared statement. It will execute the statement
with the given arguments and return the result of the closure, if one is provided.

Almost all database operations are done through this method.


Args:
        statement: The name of the prepared statement to execute and its parameters.
        args: The arguments to pass to the prepared statement. This must be a tuple.
        closure: An optional closure callback to process the result of the query. This closure will be
                                passed the cursor object and should return the result of the query.

Return:
        The result of the closure, if one is provided, or True if no closure is provided.

methods.name: acmecse.databases.PostgreSQLBinding.PostgreSQLBinding._fetchSingleRow
documentation:
Fetch the first element from the first row from the database cursor.

Args:
        cursor: The database cursor to fetch the row from.
        asList: Whether to return the row as a list or not.

Return:
        The fetched row as a single object or in a list, or None or an empty list if no row was fetched.

methods.name: acmecse.databases.PostgreSQLBinding.PostgreSQLBinding._fetchAllRows
documentation:
Fetch the first elements from all rows from the database cursor.

Args:
        cursor: The database cursor to fetch the rows from.

Return:
        The fetched rows, or an empty list if no rows were fetched.

methods.name: acmecse.databases.PostgreSQLBinding.PostgreSQLBinding._fetchNumber
documentation:
Fetch one number from the database cursor.

Args:
        cursor: The database cursor to fetch the number from.

Return:
        The fetched number, or None if no number was fetched.

classes.name: acmecse.services.SemanticManager.SemanticHandler

methods.name: acmecse.services.SemanticManager.SemanticHandler.validateDescription
documentation:
Validate a semantic description.

Args:
        description: A string with the semantic description.
        format: The format of the string in *description*. It must be supported.

Return:
        A `Result` object indicating a valid description, or with an error status.

methods.name: acmecse.services.SemanticManager.SemanticHandler.addDescription
documentation:
Add a semantic description to the graph store.

Args:
        description: A string with the semantic description.
        format: The format of the string in *description*. It must be a supported format.
        id: Identifier for the graph. It should be a resouce identifier.

Return:
        A `Result` object. The query result is returned in its *data* attribute.

methods.name: acmecse.services.SemanticManager.SemanticHandler.addParentID
documentation:
Add the parent ID to a resource's graph.

Args:
        id: Identifier for the graph. It should be a resouce identifier.
        pi: Parent ID to add.

methods.name: acmecse.services.SemanticManager.SemanticHandler.updateDescription
documentation:
Update a description in the graph store.

Args:
        description: A string with the semantic description.
        format: The format of the string in *description*.  It must be a supported format.
        id: Identifier for the graph. It should be a resouce identifier.

Return:
        A `Result` object indicating success or error.

methods.name: acmecse.services.SemanticManager.SemanticHandler.removeDescription
documentation:
Remove a description from the graph store.

Args:
        id: Identifier for the graph. It should be a resouce identifier.

Return:
        A `Result` object indicating success or error.

methods.name: acmecse.services.SemanticManager.SemanticHandler.query
documentation:
Run a SPARQL query against a graph.

Args:
        query: SPARQL query.
        ids: List of resource / graph identifiers used to build the graph for the query.
        format: Desired serialization format for the result. It must be supported.

Return:
        `Result` object. The serialized query result is stored in *data*.

methods.name: acmecse.services.SemanticManager.SemanticManager.executeSPARQLQuery
documentation:
Run a SPARQL query against a list of <`SMD`> resources.

Args:
        query: String with the SPARQL query.
        smds: A list of <`SMD`> resources, or a single <`SMD`> resource, which are to be aggregated for the query.
        ct: Result serialization format to determine the result format.

Return:
        `Result` object. If successful, the *data* attribute contains the serialized result of the query.
SMD discovery functions
def getAggregatedDescriptions(self, smds:Sequence[SMD]) -> Sequence[str]:
# TODO doc
return [ base64.decodebytes(bytes(smd.dsp, 'utf-8')).decode('utf-8')
for smd in smds ]

methods.name: acmecse.services.SemanticManager.SemanticManager.executeSemanticDiscoverySPARQLQuery
documentation:
Recursively discover link-related <`SMD`> resources and run a SPARQL query against each of the results.

This implementation support the "resource link-based" method, but not the "annotation-based" method.

When ann originator doesn't have access to a Link-related <`SMD`> resource then this resource is ignored.

Args:
        query: String with the SPARQL query.
        originator: The originator of the original request. It is used to determine the access to related resources.
        smds: A list of <`SMD`> resources which are to be aggregated and for the query. 
        ct: Result serialization format to determine the result format.

Return:
        `Result` object. If successful, the *data* attribute contains the serialized result of the query.

methods.name: acmecse.services.SemanticManager.SemanticManager._buildLinkedBasedGraphIDs
documentation:
Retrieve the resources in the *ris* attribute and follow the optional *rels* attribute recursively.

The result does not contain duplicates.

Args:
        ris: List of resource IDs to be included in the result and followed recursively.
        originator: The originator of the original request. It is used to determine the access to related resources.
        graphIDs: A dictionary of resource IDs and <`SMD`> resources that is extended during the recursive walk.

This class implements semantic service and helper functions.

Note:
        The semantic graphs are not persisted and only hold in memory at the moment.
        When the CSE is started the *SemanticManager* rebuilds the whole semantic graph
        from the existing <`SMD`> resources in the resource tree.

Attributes:
        semanticHandler: The semantic graph store handler to be used for the CSE.
        defaultFormat: Serialization format to use as a default

        The result data as a string or bytes or JSON.
def errorResultCopy(self) -> Result:
""" Copy only the rsc and dbg to a new result instance.
Return:
Result instance.
return Result(status = self.status, rsc = self.rsc, dbg = self.dbg)
@classmethod
def errorResult(cls, rsc:Optional[ResponseStatusCode] = ResponseStatusCode.BAD_REQUEST,
dbg:Optional[str] = '',
request:Optional[CSERequest] = None,
data:Optional[Any] = None) -> Result:
"""	Create and return a `Result` object with *status* set to *False* and `ResponseStatusCode` and debug
message set.
Args:
rsc: `ResponseStatusCode` to return as an error.
dbg: String with the debug message.
request: `CSERequest` to return.
Return:
Error `Result` instance.
return Result(status = False, rsc = rsc, request = request, dbg = dbg, data = data)

methods.name: acmecse.services.SecurityManager.SecurityManager.restart
documentation: Restart the Security manager service.

methods.name: acmecse.services.SecurityManager.SecurityManager.configUpdate
documentation:
Handle configuration updates.

Args:
        name: The name of the configuration section.
        key: The key of the configuration value.
        value: The new value of the configuration value.

methods.name: acmecse.services.SecurityManager.SecurityManager.hasAccess
documentation:
Test whether an originator has access to a resource for the requested permission.

Args:
        originator: The originator to check for.
        resource: The target resource of a request.
        requestedPermission: The persmission to test.
        ty: Mandatory for CREATE, else optional. The type of the resoure that is about to be created.
        parentResource: Optional, the parent resource of a target resource.
Return:
        Boolean indicating access.

methods.name: acmecse.services.SecurityManager.SecurityManager.checkAcpiUpdatePermission
documentation:
Check whether this is actually a correct update of the acpi attribute, and whether this is actually allowed.

Args:
        request: The original request.
        targetResource: The request target.
        originator: The request originator.

Return:
        Boolean value. *True* indicates that this is an ACPI update. *False* indicates that this NOT an ACPI update. if no access is provided then an exception is raised.

Raises
        `BAD_REQUEST`: If the *acpi* attribute is not the only attribute in an UPDATE request.
        `ORIGINATOR_HAS_NO_PRIVILEGE`: If the originator has no access.

methods.name: acmecse.services.SecurityManager.SecurityManager.checkSingleACPPermission
documentation:
Check whether an *originator* has the requested permissions.

Args:
        acp: The ACP resource to check.
        originator: The originator to test the permissions for.
        requestedPermission: The permissions to test.
        ty: If the resource type is given then it is checked for CREATE (as an allowed child resource type), otherwise as an allowed resource type.
        context: The context to check. Default is 'pv'.

Return:
        If any of the configured *accessControlRules* of the ACP resource matches, then the originatorhas access, and *True* is returned, or *False* otherwise. Additionally, a list of accessControlAttributes combined is returned.

methods.name: acmecse.services.SecurityManager.SecurityManager.checkACPSelfPermission
documentation:
Check whether an *originator* has the requested permissions to the `ACP` resource itself.

Args:
        originator: The originator to test the permissions for.
        requestedPermission: The permissions to test.
Return:
        If any of the configured *accessControlRules* of the ACP resource matches, then the originatorhas access, and *True* is returned, or *False* otherwise.

methods.name: acmecse.services.SecurityManager.SecurityManager._checkAcor
documentation:
Check whether an originator is in the list of acor entries.

Args:
        acp: The ACP resource to check.
        acor: The list of acor entries.
        originator: The originator to check.
        
Return:
        True if the originator is in the list of acor entries, False otherwise.

methods.name: acmecse.services.SecurityManager.SecurityManager.isAllowedOriginator
documentation:
Check whether an Originator is in the provided list of allowed originators. This list may contain regex.

The hosting CSE has always access.

Args:
        originator: The request originator.
        allowedOriginators: A list of allowed originators, which may include regex.

Return:
        Boolean value indicating the result.

methods.name: acmecse.services.SecurityManager.SecurityManager.hasAccessToPollingChannel
documentation:
Check whether the originator has access to the PCU resource.
This should be done to check the parent PCH, but the originator
would be the same as the PCU, so we can optimize this a bit.

Args:
        originator: The request originator
        resource: Either a PCH or PCU resource

Return:
        Boolean indicating the result.

methods.name: acmecse.services.SecurityManager.SecurityManager._getContext
documentation:
Depending on the configuration whether to use TLS, this method creates a new *SSLContext*
from the configured certificates and returns it. If TLS is disabled then *None* is returned.

Return:
        SSL / TLD context.
Certificate handling

methods.name: acmecse.services.SecurityManager.SecurityManager.getSSLContextHttp
documentation:
Depending on the configuration whether to use TLS, this method creates a new *SSLContext*
from the configured certificates and returns it. If TLS is disabled then *None* is returned.

This method is used for HTTP connections.

Return:
        SSL / TLD context.

methods.name: acmecse.services.SecurityManager.SecurityManager.getSSLContextCoAP
documentation:
Depending on the configuration whether to use DTLS, this method creates a new *SSLContext*
from the configured certificates and returns it. If TLS is disabled then *None* is returned.

This method is used for CoAP connections.

Return:
        SSL / TLD context.

methods.name: acmecse.services.SecurityManager.SecurityManager.getSSLContextWs
documentation:
Depending on the configuration whether to use TLS, this method creates a new *SSLContext*
from the configured certificates and returns it. If TLS is disabled then *None* is returned.

This method is used for WebSocket connections.

Return:
        SSL / TLD context.

methods.name: acmecse.services.SecurityManager.SecurityManager.validateHttpBasicAuth
documentation:
Validate the provided username and password against the configured basic authentication file.

Args:
        username: The username to validate.
        password: The password to validate.

Return:
        Boolean indicating the result.
User authentication

methods.name: acmecse.services.SecurityManager.SecurityManager.validateHttpTokenAuth
documentation:
Validate the provided token against the configured token authentication file.

Args:
        token: The token to validate.

Return:
        Boolean indicating the result.

methods.name: acmecse.services.SecurityManager.SecurityManager._readHttpBasicAuthFile
documentation:
Read the HTTP basic authentication file and store the data in a dictionary.
The authentication information is stored as username:password.

The data is stored in the `httpBasicAuthData` dictionary.

methods.name: acmecse.services.SecurityManager.SecurityManager._readHttpTokenAuthFile
documentation:
Read the HTTP token authentication file and store the data in a dictionary.
The authentication information is stored as a single token per line.

The data is stored in the `httpTokenAuthData` list.
This manager entity handles access to resources and requests.

Remove announcement details from a resource.

Modify the internal *__announcedTo__* attribute as well the *at* attribute

methods.name: acmecse.services.AnnouncementManager.AnnouncementManager._isResourceAnnouncedTo

methods.name: acmecse.services.RemoteCSEManager.RemoteCSEManager._copyCSE2CSR
documentation:
Copy the relevant attributes from a <CSEBase> to a <CSR> resource.

Args:
        target: The target <CSEBase> resource.
        source: The source <CSR> resource.
        isUpdate: Indicator that the copy operation is for an UPDATE request.

This class defines functionalities to handle remote CSE/CSR registrations.

Attributes:
        connectionMonitor: A `BackgroundWorker` that periodically checks the registrations.
        ownCSRonRegistrarCSE: The CSR resource on the registrar CSE, or None if not registered.
        registrarCSE: The registrar's CSEBase resource, or None if not registered.
        descendantCSR: A dictionary of descendant CSEs mappings: csi -> (CSR, registeredATcsi)
        enableRemoteCSE: Configuration setting. Enable or disable remote registrations.
        registrarCSEURL: The URL to the point-of-access of the registrar CSE. This is a real URL.
        registrarCSEURI: The registrar CSE's CSE-ID and resource name.
        csrOnRegistrarURI: The SP-relative ID of the CSR resource on the registrar CSE.
